title:
Lesson 1:
No Idea / Not Sure
description: 'Hiçbir fikrim yok' = I have no idea at all (emphatic). 'Fikrim yok' = I have no idea. 'Emin değilim' = I'm not sure. These express varying degrees of uncertainty.

dialogue.speaker: A
dialogue.turkish: Tren saat kaçta geliyor biliyor musun?
dialogue.english: Do you know what time the train arrives?

dialogue.speaker: B
dialogue.english: No idea at all. I didn't check.

dialogue.speaker: B
dialogue.english: I'm not sure. I think it comes at three.

dialogue.speaker: A
dialogue.turkish: Ben de fikrim yok. Telefondan bakayım.
dialogue.english: I have no idea either. Let me check on my phone.

title:
Lesson 2:
Doubt & I Knew It
description: 'Biliyordum' = I knew it. 'Şüpheliyim' = I'm doubtful / I have doubts. 'Sanmıyorum' = I don't think so. These express confidence or skepticism.

dialogue.speaker: A
dialogue.english: They say it's going to rain tomorrow.

dialogue.speaker: B
dialogue.english: I'm doubtful. Today is very sunny.

dialogue.speaker: A
dialogue.english: Don't you believe the weather forecast?

dialogue.speaker: B
dialogue.english: I don't think so. They're usually wrong.
